items.title: Expert slams WA as last state to close child safety loophole

children.content: Western Australia is the only state to not recognise Working with Children Check rejections from around the country, and has been "slow" to make other child safety law changes, expert says.

items.title: Farmers say levees built to prevent flooding have made situation worse

children.content: In Western Australia's biggest food bowl region, farmers are still assessing the damage from the latest flood caused by Cyclone Narelle. However, farmers say the levees built to reduce damage made the situation worse.

image.altText: flooded plantations in carnarvon

items.title: What's turning this red-tailed black cockatoo orange?

children.content: Spotted again in WA's South West, a rare condition is the reason for the bird's distinctive orange colouring.

image.altText: A distinctive orange and black cockatoo in a tree in Bunbury.

items.title: Federal government to back 10 renewable energy projects in regional WA

children.content: WA's energy minister says the state will have the fuel it needs for this month, as further details are released about 10 renewable energy projects that are set to be built across regional WA.

items.title: Dingo sightings near primary school put parents on edge

children.content: "Stressed-out" parents are wondering whether to let their children continue to get around unaccompanied, but wildlife advocates say the animals are not as threatening as their reputation suggests.

image.altText: A dingo glances towards the camera in the late afternoon light.

items.title: Work underway to bring plants to life after fire ravaged Stirling Range

children.content: Seed collections taken before major bushfires in WA's Stirling Range National Park give hope for the continued survival of flora species found nowhere else in the world.

image.altText: A woman standing on a mountaintop surrounded by plants

items.title: Ghost rainbows, storms and sunsets — WA's skies, captured by you

children.content: In classic shoulder season conditions, April delivered a transitional mix of warm beach weather and cool, wet days with rainfall in the double-digits as we inch closer to winter.

items.title: Man who stabbed father to death with kitchen knife not guilty due to insanity

children.content: A Perth man who fatally stabbed his father has been found not guilty of murder on the grounds of insanity.

items.title: Fatal crash on remote gravel road prompts call to limit trucks

children.content: The death of a beloved school teacher in a crash with a road train has prompted a call to review the safety of an unsealed road in remote WA.

items.title: School traffic warden hit by delivery truck in regional WA

children.content: The man in his 80s was working a school zone crossing in Australind, near Bunbury in WA, when he was hit by the Woolworths truck.

items.title: Poliovirus detected in Perth wastewater

children.content: Poliovirus has been detected in a sample taken from a wastewater in Perth, the Health Department reveals.

image.altText: am illustration of a polio virus particle against a black background

items.title: 'Can't my last days be happy?' How James Valentine faced the end

children.content: Broadcaster James Valentine was offered two different treatments for his cancer, so he chose the one that let him keep doing what he loved. Australian Story followed Valentine over his final 12 months as he faced the end with courage, purpose and humour.

items.title: King Charles won over Trump with his visit but not Zohran Mamdani

children.content: King Charles's US state visit hit a bump in New York when Mayor Zohran Mamdani said if the pair were to speak, he would "probably encourage him to return the Koh-i-Noor diamond".

image.altText: King Charles standing next to Queen Camilla leans in to listen to Zohran Mamdani.

items.title: They escaped scam compounds then saw their suffering 'gamified'

children.content: Scam centre survivors say they are hurt by games that serialise scam compound experiences online, but one creator says he hopes people find the game "fun".

items.title: It's your Sunday serve of stumpers in our spelling quiz

children.content: You lot take your spelling seriously. Repeat after me: It's just an internet quiz. It's just an internet quiz.

items.title: Rare quadruplet birth stuns mum, 45, and doctors

items.title: Australian says Gaza aid boat activists were beaten by Israeli navy

children.content: Zack Schofield was one of three Australians taken to hospital after being released by Israeli authorities, and says he saw at least 10 of his crew being shot by rubber bullets.

items.title: Gas vs beer: How Pocock caused the government a tax headache

children.content: David Pocock was struggling to find cut-through on the issue of gas taxes, but then a question about the beer excise sparked a social media war of words.

articles.title: Tie property tax changes to income tax cuts, productivity chief says

children.content: Productivity Commission chair Danielle Wood says there is a "case" for property tax changes, but for it to be serious reform, the economic benefit must flow to workers.

items.title: Hard-won legacy of Australia's longest strike in jeopardy

children.content: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

items.title: Why many Australians are heading for this island in East Asia
